reasoning: The FRED series `TMBACBW027SBOG` has exhibited a positive linear trend since April 2025 (+1.4 billion per week), but the most recent value (2720.6 on Oct 22) represents a significant local peak, sitting ~10 billion above the trailing linear trend. This suggests a high likelihood of short-term mean reversion over the first 1-2 weeks. Consequently, the probability of exceeding the Oct 26 anchor by Nov 2 is relatively low (~0.35), as the series is expected to drop back toward its 2712 trendline. For further horizons (Nov 25, Jan 24), the sustained macro upward drift driven by broader rate cut conditions gradually re-asserts itself, pushing expectations higher and bringing the probability of an increase closer to and eventually above 0.5. Following the rule that uncertainty must increase (probabilities approaching 0.5) over longer horizons, the estimates reflect this transition from short-term peak mean-reversion to longer-term drift recovery.
